definition.name: block_diagonal_algebra
definition.definition: A finite-dimensional C*-algebra A = ⊕_{i=1}^N M_{d_i}(C) (direct sum of full matrix algebras of sizes d_1, ..., d_N). States on A have the form φ ↔ (λ_1 ρ_1, ..., λ_N ρ_N) where λ_i ≥ 0 with Σ λ_i = 1 are classical weights and ρ_i is a state (density matrix) on M_{d_i}(C) for each block with λ_i > 0. The GNS representation decomposes as π_φ ≅ ⊕_{i: λ_i > 0} id_{d_i}^{⊕r_i} where r_i = rank(ρ_i). The commutant dimension is dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) = Σ_{i: λ_i > 0} r_i². The main theorem gives Σ r_i² ≤ m+1, simultaneously bounding both the ranks within each block and the number of active blocks. This "mixed Carathéodory-Barvinok-Pataki" bound appears to be new even in finite dimensions. It interpolates between pure Carathéodory (all d_i = 1) and pure Barvinok-Pataki (N = 1).
